title: Calathea arrabidae

intro: The Calathea arrabidae, also known as Calathea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, native to Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calathea include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Calathea is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory conditions, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote digestive health.
